scenarios.title: Per-run refresh fractions sum to 1.0 when every terminal node has a known policy
scenarios.rationale: RunMetricsTableWriter computes FractionFull/Incremental/NoRefresh from terminal nodes whose RefreshPolicy is one of {Full, Incremental, NoRefresh}. When the entire terminal set is recognised, the three fractions must sum to exactly 1.0 (within double-precision tolerance) — this is the metric's contract with consumers of the /summary endpoint.

scenarios.title: Terminal nodes with unknown or null RefreshPolicy are excluded from the fraction denominator
scenarios.rationale: The knownPolicies allow-list filter is the load-bearing invariant — without it, a single null/empty/unrecognised RefreshPolicy value in the terminal set would either crash the string.Equals call or silently produce fractions summing to < 1.0. The filter is the spec contract: 'fractions describe runs over the recognised-policy subset'.

scenarios.title: When no terminal nodes have a known refresh policy the fractions stay at default 0.0 with no divide-by-zero
scenarios.rationale: If terminalWithKnownPolicy.Count() is zero, the division branch must not execute. The new code path must guard the denominator; otherwise it produces NaN/Infinity values that poison the PySpark Double column and the downstream SUM(COALESCE) aggregate.

scenarios.title: FormatDouble emits values using R format with InvariantCulture so the PySpark schema stays double-typed end-to-end
scenarios.rationale: PySpark createDataFrame infers column type from the literal text. Without the 'R' round-trip format + InvariantCulture, a value like 1.0 would serialize as '1' (int) on some locales and break the DoubleType schema the writer just declared. This bug is invisible until run on a non-en-US runtime.

scenarios.title: PySpark schema declares fraction_full / fraction_incremental / fraction_no_refresh as DoubleType non-nullable
scenarios.rationale: The schema is the wire contract between the C# writer and the Spark cluster. If any of the three fraction column declarations is missing or wrongly typed, the cluster-side createDataFrame raises and the entire insights pipeline run fails.

scenarios.title: /summary InsightsSummaryResponse now carries RefreshPolicyDistribution with Full / Incremental / NoRefresh fields
scenarios.rationale: The new RefreshPolicyDistribution model + InsightsSummaryResponse property is the user-visible API contract. Forgetting to populate it produces a null on the wire and silently breaks consumers who expect the new field to be present on every /summary response.

scenarios.title: SUM(COALESCE(fraction_*, 0)) prevents pre-PR rows with NULL fractions from poisoning the aggregate
scenarios.rationale: Rows written before this PR have NULL fraction_* columns. Without COALESCE, the SUM would propagate NULL and the /summary endpoint would return null fractions for any historical window that overlaps the pre-PR period — a silent data-quality regression.

scenarios.title: /summary endpoint reads fraction columns via IsDBNullAsync guard before GetDouble
scenarios.rationale: Even with SUM(COALESCE), the reader path must individually guard each column with IsDBNullAsync — otherwise a DBNull from a partial-write scenario would throw at GetDouble. The reader hardening is a defensive boundary regardless of the SQL.
